# Lens Mount SVG Specifications

Generated by `npm run generate:mount-svgs` from `src/mounts/*.mount.ts` (schema version 1.3).
Coordinate convention: z = 0 at flange datum; +z toward lens; 0° at 12 o'clock as viewed from the camera front; clockwise positive.

## Methodology

All angles are stored in the camera-front world frame; the lens-rear view applies the mirror θ → (360 − θ) mod 360 at render time and never writes mirrored angles back. The transform is validated against the Nikon F mounting index (left-to-right reflection) in the mount test suite. 1 SVG user unit = 1 mm; each viewBox is the feature bounding box plus a 0.1 margin, rounded outward to whole mm; coordinates are rounded to 3 decimals so regeneration is byte-stable.

## MVP Policy

Headline dimensions (flange focal distance, throat, mechanism) are sourced; fine angular geometry is photo-scaled and drawn as labeled placeholders rather than guessed. Evolved mounts keep an invariant base profile plus named variant overlays; geometry is never averaged across eras.

## Mount Sections

### `bronica-etr` — Zenza Bronica ETR

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `bronica-etr` |
| Display label | Zenza Bronica ETR |
| Project note | Bronica ETR 6x4.5 medium-format SLR mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `bronica-etr/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `bronica-etr/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bronica-etr/base` | base | Bronica ETR / ETRS / ETRSi 6x4.5 leaf-shutter lenses and bodies | medium-format bayonet; leaf-shutter cocking/release coupling; aperture coupling | electrical contacts in the classic leaf-shutter lens mount | ETR-specific mapping from the broader Bronica patent family remains unconfirmed |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 69 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 54 (`patent`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 54 (`patent`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 64 (`patent`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 62 (`patent`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`patent`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`patent`) | — |

| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `bronica-etr/base` | 140° | 122° | 158° | 27 | 29.5 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `bronica-etr/base` | 260° | 242° | 278° | 27 | 29.5 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `bronica-etr/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 31.5 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `bronica-etr/base` | 270° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 28 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leaf-shutter-cocking-coupler` | both | `bronica-etr/base` | cocks/arms the lens leaf shutter |
| `aperture-control-coupler` | both | `bronica-etr/base` | body/lens aperture coordination |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 64 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 59 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -69 | 69.7 |

### `bronica-gs` — Zenza Bronica GS

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `bronica-gs` |
| Display label | Zenza Bronica GS |
| Project note | Bronica GS-1 6x7 medium-format SLR mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `bronica-gs/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `bronica-gs/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bronica-gs/base` | base | Bronica GS-1 6x7 leaf-shutter lenses and bodies | medium-format bayonet; leaf-shutter cocking/release coupling; aperture coupling | electrical contacts in the classic leaf-shutter lens mount | GS-specific mapping from the broader Bronica patent family remains unconfirmed |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leaf-shutter-cocking-coupler` | both | `bronica-gs/base` | cocks/arms the lens leaf shutter |
| `aperture-control-coupler` | both | `bronica-gs/base` | body/lens aperture coordination |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 70 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 64 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -85 | 89.6 |

#### Open questions

- GS bayonet sector geometry and leaf-shutter coupling positions are unresolved; GS and SQ share a register but are not assumed mechanically identical. _(Upgrade from Bronica GS service manuals or measured GS body/lens samples.)_

### `bronica-sq` — Zenza Bronica SQ

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `bronica-sq` |
| Display label | Zenza Bronica SQ |
| Project note | Bronica SQ 6x6 medium-format SLR mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `bronica-sq/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `bronica-sq/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bronica-sq/base` | base | Bronica SQ / SQ-A / SQ-Ai 6x6 leaf-shutter lenses and bodies | medium-format bayonet; leaf-shutter cocking/release coupling; aperture coupling | electrical contacts in the classic leaf-shutter lens mount | SQ-specific mapping from the broader Bronica patent family remains unconfirmed |

#### Camera-side front-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `body-throat` | body_throat | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 28.5 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 28.5 | 35 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `bronica-sq/base` | 20° | 2° | 38° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `bronica-sq/base` | 140° | 122° | 158° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `bronica-sq/base` | 260° | 242° | 278° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 35.5 |
| `body-lock-pin` | lock_pin | `bronica-sq/base` | 270° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 29.5 |

#### Lens-side rear-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-throat` | lens_throat | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 27.5 |
| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 27.5 | 34 |
| `lens-lug-1` | bayonet_lug | `bronica-sq/base` | 20° | 2° | 38° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `bronica-sq/base` | 140° | 122° | 158° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `bronica-sq/base` | 260° | 242° | 278° | 28.5 | 31 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `bronica-sq/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 34.5 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `bronica-sq/base` | 270° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 29.5 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leaf-shutter-cocking-coupler` | both | `bronica-sq/base` | cocks/arms the lens leaf shutter |
| `aperture-control-coupler` | both | `bronica-sq/base` | body/lens aperture coordination |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 70 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 64 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -85 | 79.2 |

| `bsq-1` | secondary | “Flange focal distance,” Wikipedia. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](http://web.archive.org/web/20260526152456/https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flange_focal_distance) | 2026-05-26 | medium |
| `bsq-2` | secondary | “Bronica SQ,” Camera-wiki.org. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://camera-wiki.org/wiki/Bronica_SQ-Ai) | 2026-06-06 | medium |
| `bsq-p1` | patent | US4063264A, Bronica lens-shutter SLR interchangeable-lens mount family. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/US4063264A/en) | 2026-06-06 | low |

#### Open questions

- SQ bayonet sector geometry and leaf-shutter coupling positions are unresolved; current figure is register-correct and separate from the GS placeholder. _(Upgrade from Bronica SQ service manuals or measured SQ body/lens samples.)_

### `canon-ef` — Canon EF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-ef` |
| Display label | Canon EF |
| Project note | Canon EF SLR mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `canon-ef/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-ef/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-ef/base` | base | all Canon EF lenses and EOS bodies, 1987–present | three unsymmetrical straight-edged bayonet prongs; locking pin/notch at 9 o'clock; red-dot mounting index; electrical contact block at the bottom (5–7 o'clock) | all mechanical aperture/AF couplings (vs the prior FD breech-lock system) | original seven contacts grew to eight on later lenses |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 44 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 65 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 63 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 8 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`secondary`) | — |

**Axial / register schematic** — viewBox `-51 -44 60 84` ([standalone SVG](mounts/canon-ef-axial.svg))

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="-51 -44 60 84" role="img" aria-labelledby="canon-ef-base-axial_section-title canon-ef-base-axial_section-desc"><title id="canon-ef-base-axial_section-title">Canon EF mount — axial / register schematic (base)</title><desc id="canon-ef-base-axial_section-desc">Canon EF bayonet mount, axial / register schematic. #### Electrical contacts

| Side | No. | Profile | Center angle | Radius | Function |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| body | 1 | `canon-ef/base` | 152° | 25 | VBAT |
| body | 2 | `canon-ef/base` | 160° | 25 | P-GND |
| body | 3 | `canon-ef/base` | 168° | 25 | VDD |
| body | 4 | `canon-ef/base` | 176° | 25 | DCL |
| body | 5 | `canon-ef/base` | 184° | 25 | DLC |
| body | 6 | `canon-ef/base` | 192° | 25 | LCLK |
| body | 7 | `canon-ef/base` | 200° | 25 | D-GND |
| body | 8 | `canon-ef/base` | 208° | 25 | COM (added on later lenses) |
| lens | 1 | `canon-ef/base` | 152° | 25 | VBAT |
| lens | 2 | `canon-ef/base` | 160° | 25 | P-GND |
| lens | 3 | `canon-ef/base` | 168° | 25 | VDD |
| lens | 4 | `canon-ef/base` | 176° | 25 | DCL |
| lens | 5 | `canon-ef/base` | 184° | 25 | DLC |
| lens | 6 | `canon-ef/base` | 192° | 25 | LCLK |
| lens | 7 | `canon-ef/base` | 200° | 25 | D-GND |
| lens | 8 | `canon-ef/base` | 208° | 25 | COM (added on later lenses) |

#### Mechanical couplings

None — fully electronic.

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 65 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 60 |
| `electrical_contact_plane` | 0.5 | 50 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -44 | 43.3 |

#### Open questions

- The EF interface began with seven contacts; an eighth was added on later lenses. contactCount records the modern eight-contact set. _(Model per-lens contact counts if a generation selector is added.)_
- Per-prong angular spans and per-contact clock positions are photo-scaled within the documented bands (lock at 9 o'clock, contacts 5–7 o'clock); the mounting-index clock position is photo-scaled. _(Upgrade to an official Canon EF mount drawing or measured sample.)_

### `canon-ef-m` — Canon EF-M

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-ef-m` |
| Display label | Canon EF-M |
| Project note | Canon EF-M APS-C mirrorless mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `canon-ef-m/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-ef-m/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-ef-m/base` | base | Canon EOS M / EF-M lenses and bodies, 2012–2023 | short-register APS-C mirrorless bayonet; three bayonet pawls; nine-contact electrical interface; lock pin/notch and mounting index | all mechanical aperture/AF couplings | 47 mm throat and 18 mm register vs EF/EF-S 54 mm and 44 mm |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 18 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 47 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 47 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 56 (`patent`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 54 (`patent`) | mm |
| Contact count | 9 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`patent`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`secondary`) | — |

#### Open questions

- EF-M bayonet span, screw PCD, lock/index clock positions, and per-contact pitch are photo-scaled from public photos/patent figures rather than production drawings. _(Upgrade to an official Canon EF-M mount drawing or measured sample.)_

### `canon-ef-s` — Canon EF-S

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-ef-s` |
| Display label | Canon EF-S |
| Project note | Canon EF-derived APS-C DSLR mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `canon-ef-s/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-ef-s/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-ef-s/base` | base | Canon EF-S APS-C DSLR lenses and compatible EOS bodies, 2003–present | EF bayonet/register geometry; white-square EF-S mounting index; rear projection/short-back-focus compatibility guard; EF-family electrical contact block | full-frame EF body compatibility for EF-S lenses | APS-C image circle and shorter permissible rear element clearance vs EF |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 44 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 65 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 63 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 8 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`secondary`) | — |

Profile canon-ef-s/base.</desc><metadata>{"mountId":"canon-ef-s","profileId":"canon-ef-s/base","view":"axial_section","schemaVersion":"1.3"}</metadata><g data-layer="datum-axis"><line x1="-44" y1="0" x2="1.2" y2="0" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.36" fill="none" /><line x1="0" y1="-32.5" x2="0" y2="32.5" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.36" fill="none" /></g><g data-layer="axial-section"><text x="-44" y="-15.675" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">sensor film plane</text><line x1="-44" y1="-14.175" x2="-44" y2="14.175" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.27" fill="none" /><line x1="0" y1="-32.5" x2="0" y2="32.5" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.36" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="0" y="-34" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">flange datum</text><line x1="0.5" y1="-25" x2="0.5" y2="25" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.36" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="0.5" y="-26.5" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">electrical contact plane</text><line x1="1.2" y1="-30" x2="1.2" y2="30" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.36" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="1.2" y="-31.5" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">bayonet lug engagement</text></g><g aria-hidden="true"><line x1="-49.8" y1="-39.8" x2="-45.8" y2="-39.8" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.588" /><text x="-44.456" y="-38.859" font-size="2.688" fill="#8a94a0" font-family="sans-serif">Datum &amp; axis</text><line x1="-49.8" y1="-35.768" x2="-45.8" y2="-35.768" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.588" /><text x="-44.456" y="-34.827" font-size="2.688" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">Register plane</text></g></svg>

| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `canon-ef-s/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 27 | 32.5 |

#### Electrical contacts

| Side | No. | Profile | Center angle | Radius | Function |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| body | 1 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 152° | 25 | VBAT |
| body | 2 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 160° | 25 | P-GND |
| body | 3 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 168° | 25 | VDD |
| body | 4 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 176° | 25 | DCL |
| body | 5 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 184° | 25 | DLC |
| body | 6 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 192° | 25 | LCLK |
| body | 7 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 200° | 25 | D-GND |
| body | 8 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 208° | 25 | COM |
| lens | 1 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 152° | 25 | VBAT |
| lens | 2 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 160° | 25 | P-GND |
| lens | 3 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 168° | 25 | VDD |
| lens | 4 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 176° | 25 | DCL |
| lens | 5 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 184° | 25 | DLC |
| lens | 6 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 192° | 25 | LCLK |
| lens | 7 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 200° | 25 | D-GND |
| lens | 8 | `canon-ef-s/base` | 208° | 25 | COM |

#### Mechanical couplings

None — fully electronic.

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 65 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 60 |
| `electrical_contact_plane` | 0.5 | 50 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -44 | 28.35 |

#### Open questions

- EF-S shares the EF bayonet/register envelope, but the rear projection and exact white-square/index geometry are not dimensioned by the available sources. _(Upgrade with an official EF-S body/lens mount drawing or measured EF-S sample.)_

### `canon-fd` — Canon FD

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-fd` |
| Display label | Canon FD |
| Project note | Canon FD manual-focus SLR breech-lock mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `breech_lock` (lock `breech_collar`) |
| Base profile | `canon-fd/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-fd/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-fd/base` | base | Canon FD breech-lock lenses and bodies, 1971–1979 | FD/FL breech-lock bayonet; lens-side rotating locking collar; stop-down lever; aperture indicator lever | electrical contacts | adds selected-aperture reporting over the earlier FL interface |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 42 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 48 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 48 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 58 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 56 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 90 (`secondary`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`secondary`) | — |

**Axial / register schematic** — viewBox `-49 -40 57 76` ([standalone SVG](mounts/canon-fd-axial.svg))

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="-49 -40 57 76" role="img" aria-labelledby="canon-fd-base-axial_section-title canon-fd-base-axial_section-desc"><title id="canon-fd-base-axial_section-title">Canon FD mount — axial / register schematic (base)</title><desc id="canon-fd-base-axial_section-desc">Canon FD breech-lock mount, axial / register schematic. #### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `stop-down-lever` | both | `canon-fd/base` | camera stops the aperture down at exposure |
| `aperture-indicator-lever` | both | `canon-fd/base` | reports selected aperture from lens to body |
| `breech-lock-collar` | lens | `canon-fd/base` | clamps the lens to the body without rotating the lens barrel |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 58 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 53 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -42 | 43.3 |

#### Open questions

- FD/FL breech-lock sector spans, collar ramp details, and lever clock positions are photo-scaled; the patent leads are related mechanism patents, not a full production mount drawing. _(Upgrade to Canon service drawings or measured FD body/lens samples.)_

### `canon-fl` — Canon FL

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-fl` |
| Display label | Canon FL |
| Project note | Canon FL manual-focus SLR breech-lock mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `breech_lock` (lock `breech_collar`) |
| Base profile | `canon-fl/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-fl/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-fl/base` | base | Canon FL breech-lock lenses and bodies, 1964–1971 | FL/FD-family breech-lock bayonet; lens-side rotating locking collar; automatic diaphragm lever | FD selected-aperture reporting linkage; electrical contacts | predecessor to FD, with simpler aperture communication |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 42 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 48 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 48 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 58 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 56 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 90 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

#### Camera-side front-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `body-throat` | body_throat | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 24 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 24 | 29 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `canon-fl/base` | 30° | 10° | 50° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `canon-fl/base` | 150° | 130° | 170° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `canon-fl/base` | 270° | 250° | 290° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 29.5 |
| `body-lock-pin` | lock_pin | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 25 |

#### Lens-side rear-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-throat` | lens_throat | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 23 |
| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 23 | 28 |
| `lens-lug-1` | bayonet_lug | `canon-fl/base` | 30° | 10° | 50° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `canon-fl/base` | 150° | 130° | 170° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `canon-fl/base` | 270° | 250° | 290° | 24 | 26.5 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 28.5 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `canon-fl/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 25 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `automatic-diaphragm-lever` | both | `canon-fl/base` | automatic diaphragm stop-down |
| `breech-lock-collar` | lens | `canon-fl/base` | clamps the lens to the body without rotating the lens barrel |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 58 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 53 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -42 | 43.3 |

#### Open questions

- FL-specific lug/collar geometry and diaphragm-lever position are unresolved; current diagram is a renderable FL-family approximation. _(Upgrade to Canon FL service drawings or measured body/lens samples.)_

### `canon-rf` — Canon RF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `canon-rf` |
| Display label | Canon RF |
| Project note | Canon RF mirrorless mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `canon-rf/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `canon-rf/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `canon-rf/base` | base | all Canon RF lenses and EOS R bodies (full-frame and RF-S), 2018–present | three bayonet tabs; locking pin/notch at 9 o'clock; red-dot mounting index; twelve-contact electrical block at the bottom (5–7 o'clock), tiered | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | denser 12-pin interface vs EF's 8 |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 20 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 54 (`official`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 65 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 63 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 12 (`official`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |
| Lock direction | unknown (`unknown`) | — |

### `contax-rf` — Contax RF

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `contax-rf` |
| Display label | Contax RF |
| Project note | Contax rangefinder bayonet with internal/external focusing interfaces. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `hybrid` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `contax-rf/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `contax-rf/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `contax-rf/base` | base | Zeiss Ikon Contax rangefinder lenses and bodies | internal bayonet for normal lenses; external bayonet for larger lenses; rangefinder focusing coupling | SLR mirror clearance | internal/external bayonet details are collapsed into one renderable MVP profile |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 34.85 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 52 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 45 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `exakta/base` | base | inner Exakta bayonet lenses and bodies, 1936 onward | three-pronged inner bayonet; spring-loaded locking/release pin; left-side release clearance | electrical contacts | external telephoto bayonet and later Topcon/Mamiya variants not yet modeled |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `body-throat` | body_throat | `exakta/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 16.9 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `exakta/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 16.9 | 21.5 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `exakta/base` | 0° | 343° | 17° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `exakta/base` | 120° | 103° | 137° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `exakta/base` | 240° | 223° | 257° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `exakta/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 22 |
| `body-lock-pin` | lock_pin | `exakta/base` | 300° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 17.9 |

#### Lens-side rear-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-throat` | lens_throat | `exakta/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 15.899999999999999 |
| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `exakta/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 15.899999999999999 | 20.5 |
| `lens-lug-1` | bayonet_lug | `exakta/base` | 0° | 343° | 17° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `exakta/base` | 120° | 103° | 137° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `exakta/base` | 240° | 223° | 257° | 16.9 | 19.4 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `exakta/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 21 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `exakta/base` | 300° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 17.9 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-release-lever-clearance` | body | `exakta/base` | retains and releases the lens locking pin |
| `aperture-pod-pass-through` | lens | `exakta/base` | allows Exakta auto-aperture pod lenses to actuate the body release |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 43 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 38 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -44.7 | 43.3 |

### `fixed-lens-camera` — Fixed-lens Camera

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `fixed-lens-camera` |
| Display label | Fixed-lens Camera |
| Project note | Not an interchangeable mount; rendered as an integral lens/sensor schematic. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `not_applicable` |
| Mount mechanism | `fixed_lens` (lock `not_applicable`) |
| Base profile | `fixed-lens-camera/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `fixed-lens-camera/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fixed-lens-camera/base` | base | Cameras whose lens is permanently integrated with the body | integral lens module datum | interchangeable mount throat; bayonet lugs; thread; contacts | dimensions are deliberately not a generic mount standard |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

#### Mechanical couplings

None — fully electronic.

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `lens_module_datum` | 0 | 44 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -8 | 28 |

#### Open questions

- Fixed-lens cameras do not share a standardized interchangeable mount; the diagram is a not-applicable placeholder. _(Create per-camera internal module drawings only when a fixed-lens camera has service-level data.)_

### `four-thirds` — Four Thirds

|---|---|
| Mount ID | `four-thirds` |
| Display label | Four Thirds |
| Project note | Olympus/Panasonic Four Thirds DSLR mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `four-thirds/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `four-thirds/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `four-thirds/base` | base | Four Thirds DSLR lenses and bodies, 2003–2017 | three-pronged bayonet; 9-contact electronic interface; 9 o'clock lock groove | mechanical aperture/AF couplings | — |

| Lens mount outer diameter | 48 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 9 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

| `fujifilm-g/base` | base | all Fujifilm G/GFX lenses and bodies (44×33 medium format), 2017–present | three bayonet tabs; locking pin/notch at 4:30; mounting index; electrical block at the bottom | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | — |

#### Open questions

- Official Fujifilm material confirms twelve electronic contacts, while the JAPB teardown counts eleven visible contacts; the twelfth contact's production clocking remains schematic. _(Keep the official twelve-contact count; upgrade per-contact positions from a service drawing or measured sample.)_
- Tab angular spans, the mounting-index clock position, and the lock rotation angle/direction are photo-scaled or undocumented; per-contact clock positions are interpolated within the documented 5–7 o'clock band. _(Upgrade to an official Fujifilm G mount drawing or measured sample.)_

### `fujifilm-x` — Fujifilm X

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `fujifilm-x` |
| Display label | Fujifilm X |
| Project note | Fujifilm X APS-C mirrorless mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `fujifilm-x/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `fujifilm-x/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fujifilm-x/base` | base | all Fujifilm X lenses and bodies (APS-C), 2012–present | three straight-edged bayonet lugs; locking pin/notch at 4:30; mounting index; ten-contact electrical block at the bottom (4:30–7:30) | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | — |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 17.7 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 48 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 10 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |
| Lock direction | unknown (`unknown`) | — |

#### Mechanical couplings

None — fully electronic.

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 50 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1 | 46 |
| `electrical_contact_plane` | 0.5 | 42 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -17.7 | 28.3 |

| Display label | Hasselblad H |
| Project note | Hasselblad H-system medium-format autofocus bayonet mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `hasselblad-h/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `hasselblad-h/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hasselblad-h/base` | base | Hasselblad H-system lenses and bodies | large medium-format bayonet; electrical contact bank; leaf-shutter communication | — | renderable profile uses chart dimensions and photo-scaled feature placement |

| Lens mount outer diameter | 78 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 10 (`photo_scaled`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |

### `hasselblad-v` — Hasselblad V

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `hasselblad-v` |
| Display label | Hasselblad V |
| Project note | Hasselblad V-system medium-format bayonet mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `hasselblad-v/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `hasselblad-v/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hasselblad-v/base` | base | Hasselblad V-system 500/200-series lenses and bodies | medium-format bayonet interface; lens shutter synchronization/cocking couplings | electrical contacts in the classic mechanical interface | early Hasselblad-related patent is treated as related lineage, not a complete V mount drawing |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 74.9 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 69 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 69 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 78 (`patent`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 76 (`patent`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`patent`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`patent`) | — |

| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 34.5 | 39 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | 343° | 17° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `hasselblad-v/base` | 120° | 103° | 137° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `hasselblad-v/base` | 240° | 223° | 257° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 39.5 |
| `body-lock-pin` | lock_pin | `hasselblad-v/base` | 270° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 35.5 |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 33.5 | 38 |
| `lens-lug-1` | bayonet_lug | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | 343° | 17° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `hasselblad-v/base` | 120° | 103° | 137° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `hasselblad-v/base` | 240° | 223° | 257° | 34.5 | 37 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `hasselblad-v/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 38.5 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `hasselblad-v/base` | 270° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 35.5 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `leaf-shutter-cocking-coupler` | both | `hasselblad-v/base` | coordinates the lens leaf shutter with the body |
| `aperture-shutter-release-coupler` | both | `hasselblad-v/base` | body-to-lens shutter/aperture release coordination |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 78 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 72 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -74.9 | 79.2 |

|---|---|
| Mount ID | `l-mount` |
| Display label | L-Mount |

| Base profile | `l-mount/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `l-mount/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `l-mount/base` | base | all L-Mount lenses and bodies (APS-C TL and full-frame SL), 2014–present | four bayonet lugs; locking pin/notch at 4:30; mounting index; ten-contact electrical block at the top (11–1 o'clock) | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | — |

| Lens mount outer diameter | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 10 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |

Profile l-mount/base.</desc><metadata>{"mountId":"l-mount","profileId":"l-mount/base","view":"axial_section","schemaVersion":"1.3"}</metadata><g data-layer="datum-axis"><line x1="-20" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.25" fill="none" /><line x1="0" y1="-31" x2="0" y2="31" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.25" fill="none" /></g><g data-layer="axial-section"><text x="-20" y="-23.15" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">sensor film plane</text><line x1="-20" y1="-21.65" x2="-20" y2="21.65" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.188" fill="none" /><line x1="0" y1="-31" x2="0" y2="31" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.25" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="0" y="-32.5" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">flange datum</text><line x1="0.5" y1="-24.5" x2="0.5" y2="24.5" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.25" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="0.5" y="-26" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">electrical contact plane</text><line x1="1" y1="-27.5" x2="1" y2="27.5" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.25" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="4 2" /><text x="1" y="-29" font-size="2.4" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">bayonet lug engagement</text></g><g aria-hidden="true"><line x1="-26.3" y1="-38" x2="-22.3" y2="-38" stroke="#8a94a0" stroke-width="0.56" /><text x="-21.02" y="-37.104" font-size="2.56" fill="#8a94a0" font-family="sans-serif">Datum &amp; axis</text><line x1="-26.3" y1="-34.16" x2="-22.3" y2="-34.16" stroke="#2bb0c4" stroke-width="0.56" /><text x="-21.02" y="-33.264" font-size="2.56" fill="#2bb0c4" font-family="sans-serif">Register plane</text></g></svg>

#### Mechanical couplings

None — fully electronic.

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 62 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1 | 55 |
| `electrical_contact_plane` | 0.5 | 49 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -20 | 43.3 |

#### Open questions

- Thread start clocking is not meaningful across generic screw-mount bodies and is diagrammed representatively. _(Keep as representative unless adding a specific camera-body thread-start variant.)_

### `leica-m` — Leica M

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `leica-m` |
| Display label | Leica M |
| Project note | Leica M bayonet rangefinder mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `leica-m/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `leica-m/6-bit` |
| Variant strategy | `variant_profiles_defined` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `leica-m/base` | base | all Leica M lenses and bodies, 1954–present | four bayonet lugs with one keyed lug/slot pair; tactile orientation notch/protuberance; mounting index; rangefinder-lever access / selected-lug control cam | electrical contacts (rangefinder mechanical interface) | — |
| `leica-m/6-bit` | variant | 6-bit-coded M lenses (digital M era) | 6-bit lens-identification coding (six white/black marks on the flange) | — | camera reads lens identity optically |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 27.8 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 52 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |
| Lock direction | unknown (`unknown`) | — |

#### Camera-side front-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `body-throat` | body_throat | `leica-m/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 22 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `leica-m/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 22 | 26 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `leica-m/base` | 45° | 23° | 67° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `leica-m/base` | 135° | 121° | 149° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `leica-m/base` | 225° | 211° | 239° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `body-slot-4` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `leica-m/base` | 315° | 301° | 329° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `leica-m/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 26.5 |
| `body-lock-pin` | alignment_pin | `leica-m/base` | 45° | 45° | 45° | 24.5 | 24.5 |

#### Lens-side rear-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-throat` | lens_throat | `leica-m/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 21 |
| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `leica-m/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 21 | 25.5 |
| `lens-lug-1` | bayonet_lug | `leica-m/base` | 45° | 23° | 67° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `lens-lug-2` | bayonet_lug | `leica-m/base` | 135° | 121° | 149° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `lens-lug-3` | bayonet_lug | `leica-m/base` | 225° | 211° | 239° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `lens-lug-4` | bayonet_lug | `leica-m/base` | 315° | 301° | 329° | 22 | 25.5 |
| `lens-index-mark` | index_mark | `leica-m/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | unknown | 26 |
| `lens-lock-notch` | lock_notch | `leica-m/base` | 45° | 45° | 45° | 24.5 | 24.5 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `rangefinder-coupling-cam` | both | `leica-m/base` | allows rangefinder-lever access and selected-lug control-cam actuation |
| `six-bit-coding` | lens | `leica-m/6-bit` | optical lens-identification coding |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 52 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1 | 48 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -27.8 | 43.3 |

#### Open questions

- US2618201A supports the four-lug bayonet, keyed lug/slot, tactile notch/protuberance, selected-lug cam, and rangefinder-lever access recess; exact Leica M production lug clocking/spans, production sprung-lock details, the mounting-index clock position, and 6-bit-coding positions still need an official drawing or measured sample. _(Upgrade to an official Leica M mount drawing or measured sample.)_

### `leica-r` — Leica R

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `leica-r` |
| Display label | Leica R |
| Project note | Leica R SLR bayonet mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |

| Selected MVP profile | `leica-r/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `leica-r/base` | base | Leica R SLR lenses and bodies | three-lug bayonet; mechanical aperture/metering cam interface | rangefinder coupling; threaded LTM interface | variant cam and ROM details are not split into profile overlays yet |

| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

| `lr-1` | secondary | “Flange focal distance,” Wikipedia. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](http://web.archive.org/web/20260526152456/https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flange_focal_distance) | 2026-05-26 | medium |
| `lr-2` | secondary | Leica R mount visual references. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](unknown) | unknown | low |

#### Open questions

- Leica R cam variants and exact lug profile need measured drawings. _(Split R cam/ROM generations after sourcing service documentation.)_

### `m42` — M42

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `m42` |
| Display label | M42 |
| Project note | M42 x 1 screw mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `threaded` (lock `friction_shoulder`) |
| Base profile | `m42/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `m42/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `m42/base` | base | M42 x 1 SLR screw-mount lenses and bodies | 42 mm screw thread; automatic aperture stop-down pin on common variants | bayonet lugs; electrical contacts | — |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 45.46 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 42 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 42 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 48 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 360 (`secondary`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`secondary`) | — |

| `body-thread-throat` | body_throat | `m42/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 21 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `m42/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 21 | 25 |
| `body-thread-start` | thread_start | `m42/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | 20 | 21 |

#### Lens-side rear-view geometry

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lens-throat` | lens_throat | `m42/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 20 |
| `lens-thread` | male_thread | `m42/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 20 | 21 |
| `lens-mount-ring` | lens_mount_ring | `m42/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 21 | 24 |
| `lens-thread-start` | thread_start | `m42/base` | 0° | unknown | unknown | 20 | 21 |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aperture-stop-down-pin` | lens | `m42/base` | stops the lens aperture down on automatic M42 lenses |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 50 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 42 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -45.46 | 43.3 |

### `micro-four-thirds` — Micro Four Thirds

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `micro-four-thirds` |
| Display label | Micro Four Thirds |
| Project note | Micro Four Thirds mirrorless mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `micro-four-thirds/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `micro-four-thirds/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `micro-four-thirds/base` | base | Micro Four Thirds lenses and bodies, 2008–present | three-pronged bayonet; 11-contact electronic interface; 3 o'clock lock groove | mechanical aperture/AF couplings | shorter register and two additional contacts vs Four Thirds |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 19.25 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 38 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 38 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 44 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 42 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 11 (`secondary`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

- Micro Four Thirds lug spans, contact pitch and screw positions are photo-scaled within the documented lock/contact bands. _(Upgrade to the MFT standard drawing or measured body/lens sample.)_

### `minolta-sr` — Minolta SR

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `minolta-sr` |
| Display label | Minolta SR |
| Project note | Minolta SR manual-focus SLR bayonet mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `minolta-sr/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `minolta-sr/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `minolta-sr/base` | base | Minolta SR, MC, and MD manual-focus lenses and bodies | three-lug bayonet; aperture and meter coupling levers | electrical contacts | SR/MC/MD couplings are collapsed for MVP rendering |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 43.5 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 45 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 45 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 55 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 53 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aperture-actuation-lever` | both | `minolta-sr/base` | stops down aperture at exposure |
| `meter-coupling-tab` | lens | `minolta-sr/base` | reports aperture setting to body |

  "coordinateConvention": {

    "storedAngleFrame": "camera_front_view",
    "lensRearMirror": "theta_out = (360 - theta) mod 360",
    "angleSpanRule": "clockwise_sweep_start_to_end_mod_360"
  },
  "render": {
    "unitMapping": "1_user_unit_per_mm",
    "marginFraction": 0.1,
    "numericPrecision": 3,
    "views": {
      "cameraSideFront": {
        "viewBox": "-34 -35 68 69"
      },
      "lensSideRear": {
        "viewBox": "-33 -34 66 67"
      },
      "axialSection": {
        "viewBox": "-50 -38 58 72"
      }
    }
  },
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Minolta SR mount",
    "desc": "Minolta SR mount — flange focal distance 43.5 mm, nominal throat 45 mm.",
    "view": "unknown",
    "profileId": "minolta-sr/base"
  }
}
```

#### Open questions

- Nikon 1 throat/outer diameters, lug spans and screw positions are photo-scaled; contact bank placement follows the documented 10:30–1:30 band. _(Upgrade to a Nikon service drawing or measured Nikon 1 body/lens mount sample.)_

### `nikon-f` — Nikon F

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `nikon-f` |
| Display label | Nikon F |
| Project note | Nikon F SLR mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `nikon-f/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `nikon-f/af-i-af-s` |
| Variant strategy | `variant_profiles_defined` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `nikon-f/base` | base | all Nikon F bodies and lenses, 1959–present | three non-symmetric bayonet lugs; lock pin/notch; mounting index; diaphragm lever | — | — |
| `nikon-f/non-ai` | variant | pre-AI ('F') lenses, 1959–1977 | metering coupling prong ('rabbit ears') / coupling shoe | — | maximum-aperture metering set by the prong/shoe |
| `nikon-f/ai` | variant | AI (Automatic Indexing) lenses, 1977+ | meter coupling ridge; lens speed indexing post; aperture direct readout (ADR) prong | — | maximum-aperture indexing automated vs the pre-AI prong |
| `nikon-f/ai-s` | variant | AI-S lenses, 1981+ (renders the AI/AI-S meter-coupling ridge introduced by AI) | AI-S lens-type signal notch; focal-length indexing ridge | — | linear aperture actuation for shutter-priority / program AE |
| `nikon-f/af-d` | variant | AF / AF-D screw-drive autofocus lenses | AF screw-drive coupler; five CPU contacts ('4+1'); subject-distance (D) encoding | — | electronic aperture/distance reporting added |
| `nikon-f/af-i-af-s` | variant | AF-I, AF-S and AF-P in-lens-motor lenses (incl. G and E aperture variants) | in-lens AF motor (AF-I coreless / AF-S SWM / AF-P stepping); CPU contact block (eight-contact representative; up to ten across later variants) | — | body AF screw coupler unused; AF driven in the lens; AF-P incompatible with many older bodies |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 46.5 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 54 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 8 (`photo_scaled`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 60 (`patent`) | deg |
| Lock direction | counterclockwise (`secondary`) | — |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `metering-prong` | both | `nikon-f/non-ai` | pre-AI maximum-aperture metering coupling |
| `ai-meter-coupling-ridge` | both | `nikon-f/ai-s` | open-aperture metering coupling (AI/AI-S) |
| `lens-speed-indexing-post` | body | `nikon-f/ai-s` | maximum-aperture indexing |
| `diaphragm-driving-lever` | both | `nikon-f/base` | mechanical diaphragm actuation |
| `af-screw-coupler` | both | `nikon-f/af-d` | mechanical AF drive |

  "openQuestions": [
    {
      "issue": "Bayonet lug/slot spans now carry patent-embodiment provenance, but exact production F-mount spans and the mounting-index clock position still need an official Nikon drawing or measured sample.",
      "affectedFields": [
        "cameraSideFeatures",
        "lensSideFeatures",
        "body-index-mark",
        "lens-index-mark"
      ],
      "candidateValues": [],
      "resolution": "Upgrade to an official Nikon mount drawing or measured sample."
    },
    {
      "issue": "US4766453A supports the five-contact AF-D embodiment; US5185622A supports a later multi-contact connector layout but not the production per-contact service functions. AF-I/AF-S/AF-P carry up to ten contacts, while the eight-contact set modeled here remains representative.",
      "affectedFields": [
        "contacts",
        "contactCount"
      ],
      "candidateValues": [
        5,
        7,
        8,
        10
      ],
      "resolution": "Model per-variant contact maps from an official Nikon service reference."
    }

| `nf-1` | secondary | “Nikon F-mount,” Wikipedia. Accessed 2026-06-04. | [snapshot](http://web.archive.org/web/20260513020722/https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nikon_F-mount) | 2026-05-13 | high |
| `nf-p1` | patent | US4766453A, Nikon AF-era F-mount bayonet/drive/contact arrangement. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/US4766453A/en) | 2026-06-06 | high |
| `nf-p2` | patent | US5185622A, Nikon AF-era F-mount/contact arrangement. Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/US5185622A/en) | 2026-06-06 | high |
| `nf-photo-1` | field_photo | User-supplied rear view of an AF-S G-type Nikon F lens (IMG_1872.jpeg), 2026-06-06. | [snapshot]() | unknown | high |

#### Open questions

- Bayonet lug/slot spans now carry patent-embodiment provenance, but exact production F-mount spans and the mounting-index clock position still need an official Nikon drawing or measured sample. _(Upgrade to an official Nikon mount drawing or measured sample.)_
- US4766453A supports the five-contact AF-D embodiment; US5185622A supports a later multi-contact connector layout but not the production per-contact service functions. AF-I/AF-S/AF-P carry up to ten contacts, while the eight-contact set modeled here remains representative. _(Model per-variant contact maps from an official Nikon service reference.)_

### `nikon-s` — Nikon S

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `nikon-s` |
| Display label | Nikon S |
| Project note | Nikon S rangefinder bayonet mount. |
| Research status | `partial` |
| MVP status | `renderable` |
| Mount mechanism | `hybrid` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `nikon-s/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `nikon-s/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `nikon-s/base` | base | Nikon S rangefinder lenses and bodies | internal/external rangefinder bayonet; rangefinder focusing coupling | SLR mirror clearance | inner/outer bayonet detail is simplified for MVP rendering |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 34.85 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 52 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 50 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | not_applicable (`not_applicable`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 45 (`photo_scaled`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`photo_scaled`) | — |

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `nikon-z` |
| Display label | Nikon Z |
| Project note | Nikon Z mirrorless mount. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `nikon-z/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `nikon-z/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `nikon-z/base` | base | all Nikon Z lenses and bodies (FX and DX), 2018–present | four straight-edged bayonet lugs; locking pin/notch at 3 o'clock; mounting index; eleven-contact electrical block at the top (11–1 o'clock) | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | — |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 16 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 55 (`official`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 55 (`official`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 66 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 64 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 11 (`patent`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |
| Lock direction | unknown (`unknown`) | — |

#### Open questions

- US10831084B2 supports the four-claw bayonet, lock-pin/receiving-groove relationship, and 11-terminal order, but it is a patent embodiment rather than an official Nikon service drawing; absolute production lug spans, mounting-index clock position, lock rotation angle/direction, and screw positions remain schematic/photo-scaled. _(Upgrade to an official Nikon Z mount drawing or measured sample.)_
- JAPB describes the Nikon Z contact bank at 5-7 o'clock, while US10831084B2 places both body-side and lens-side terminal holding units in the upper portion and shows the patent terminal order on the upper arc. The MVP follows the Nikon patent for contact clocking. _(Keep the patent upper-arc placement unless an official Nikon service drawing supersedes it.)_

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `olympus-om/base` | base | all Olympus OM manual-focus lenses and bodies, 1972–2002 | bayonet lugs; lock pin/notch; mounting index; automatic-diaphragm aperture coupling | electrical contacts (mechanical manual-focus interface) | — |

#### Open questions

- Olympus OM lacks an archived teardown with clock positions: the throat diameter, bayonet lug count, the aperture-coupling position, and the lock/index clock positions are photo-scaled. _(Upgrade to an official Olympus OM mount drawing or measured sample.)_

#### Open questions

- Pentax 67 throat, lug sectors, and aperture lever clocking are photo-scaled. _(Upgrade with measured Pentax 67 body/lens samples.)_

### `pentax-k` — Pentax K

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `pentax-k` |
| Display label | Pentax K |
| Project note | Pentax K mount family. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `pentax-k/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `pentax-k/ka` |
| Variant strategy | `variant_profiles_defined` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `pentax-k/base` | base | all Pentax K bodies and lenses, 1975–present | three-lug bayonet (two straight edges); lock pin/notch; mounting index | — | — |
| `pentax-k/k` | variant | original K (M-series era) lenses, 1975+ | aperture stop-down coupler; diaphragm release lever | — | mechanical aperture interface; KA retains these levers (rendered under the KA figure) |
| `pentax-k/ka` | variant | KA (A-series) bodies and lenses, 1983+ | six A-position information contacts (m1, m2, r1, r2, r3, *) | — | aperture automation via the contacts; retains the original mechanical levers |
| `pentax-k/kaf` | variant | KAF autofocus bodies and lenses | AF screw-drive coupler; seventh digital information contact | — | — |
| `pentax-k/kaf2` | variant | KAF2 power-zoom bodies and lenses | two power contacts inside the mounting ring | — | — |
| `pentax-k/kaf3` | variant | KAF3 lenses (SDM/DC in-lens motor) | — | body AF screw-drive coupler | autofocus driven in the lens |
| `pentax-k/kaf4` | variant | KAF4 lenses (electromagnetic diaphragm) | electromagnetic diaphragm control | mechanical aperture (diaphragm) lever | aperture actuated electronically |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 45.46 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 44 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 56 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 52 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 6 (`patent`) | count |
| Lock rotation | 70 (`secondary`) | deg |
| Lock direction | clockwise (`patent`) | — |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `aperture-stop-down-coupler` | body | `pentax-k/ka` | sense aperture-ring setting |
| `diaphragm-release-lever` | body | `pentax-k/ka` | hold open spring-loaded diaphragm |
| `af-screw-coupler` | body | `pentax-k/kaf` | mechanical AF drive |
| `af-driven-shaft` | lens | `pentax-k/kaf` | mechanical AF drive receiver |
| `af-screw-coupler-kaf2` | body | `pentax-k/kaf2` | mechanical AF drive |
| `af-driven-shaft-kaf2` | lens | `pentax-k/kaf2` | mechanical AF drive receiver |

#### Open questions

- US4017878A supports the three-claw bayonet/lock embodiment, US4357089A supports the KA opposed contact bank, and US4939532A supports the AF joint shaft plus data/power contacts; exact production throat, lug spans, contact pad dimensions/clocking, and later KAF/KAF2 terminal functions remain normalized to secondary imagery. _(Upgrade to an official Pentax/Ricoh K-mount drawing or measured sample.)_
- Contact count grows across KA (6) → KAF (7) → KAF2 (9); contactCount records the KA baseline. _(Expose per-variant contact counts when variant selection drives the figure.)_

### `sigma-sa` — Sigma SA

|---|---|

| Project note | Sigma SA autofocus SLR/DSLR bayonet mount. |

| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `sigma-sa/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `sigma-sa/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sony-a/base` | base | all Minolta AF / Sony A lenses and bodies (full-frame and APS-C DT), 1985-present | bayonet lugs; lock pin/notch; mounting index; AF screw-drive coupler; mechanical aperture-control arm; electrical contact block | — | original Minolta 5-pin interface grew to eight contacts |

| Feature ID | Type | Profile | Center | Start | End | Inner r | Outer r |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `body-throat` | body_throat | `sony-a/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 0 | 24.85 |
| `body-mount-ring` | mount_ring | `sony-a/base` | 0° | 0° | 360° | 24.85 | 28.5 |
| `body-slot-1` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `sony-a/base` | 30° | 10° | 50° | 24.85 | 27.5 |
| `body-slot-2` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `sony-a/base` | 150° | 130° | 170° | 24.85 | 27.5 |
| `body-slot-3` | bayonet_receiving_slot | `sony-a/base` | 290° | 270° | 310° | 24.85 | 27.5 |
| `body-index-mark` | index_mark | `sony-a/base` | 0° | 0° | 0° | 28 | 29 |
| `body-lock-pin` | lock_pin | `sony-a/base` | 270° | 270° | 270° | 25.5 | 25.5 |

#### Electrical contacts

| Side | No. | Profile | Center angle | Radius | Function |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| body | 1 | `sony-a/base` | 152° | 22.5 | circuit power |
| body | 2 | `sony-a/base` | 160° | 22.5 | motor power |
| body | 3 | `sony-a/base` | 168° | 22.5 | motor ground |
| body | 4 | `sony-a/base` | 176° | 22.5 | lens/circuit ground |
| body | 5 | `sony-a/base` | 184° | 22.5 | serial data output |
| body | 6 | `sony-a/base` | 192° | 22.5 | communication request |
| body | 7 | `sony-a/base` | 200° | 22.5 | serial clock |
| body | 8 | `sony-a/base` | 208° | 22.5 | serial data input |
| lens | 1 | `sony-a/base` | 152° | 22.5 | circuit power |
| lens | 2 | `sony-a/base` | 160° | 22.5 | motor power |
| lens | 3 | `sony-a/base` | 168° | 22.5 | motor ground |
| lens | 4 | `sony-a/base` | 176° | 22.5 | lens/circuit ground |
| lens | 5 | `sony-a/base` | 184° | 22.5 | serial data output |
| lens | 6 | `sony-a/base` | 192° | 22.5 | communication request |
| lens | 7 | `sony-a/base` | 200° | 22.5 | serial clock |
| lens | 8 | `sony-a/base` | 208° | 22.5 | serial data input |

#### Mechanical couplings

| Feature | Side | Profile | Function |
|---|---|---|---|
| `af-screw-coupler` | body | `sony-a/base` | mechanical (screw-drive) AF |
| `aperture-control-arm` | body | `sony-a/base` | mechanical aperture actuation |
| `lens-aperture-pin` | lens | `sony-a/base` | lens diaphragm pin actuated by the body aperture control member |

#### Axial stack

| Plane | z (mm) | Diameter (mm) |
|---|---|---|
| `flange_datum` | 0 | 57 |
| `bayonet_lug_engagement` | 1.2 | 52 |
| `electrical_contact_plane` | 0.5 | 46 |
| `sensor_film_plane` | -44.5 | 43.3 |

      "appliesTo": "Minolta/Sony A-style body/lens mount faces, three locking claws, clockwise engagement, index, eight body/lens contacts 15a-15h/25a-25h, lock pin/hole, and contact/claw overlap",
      "confidence": "high"
    }
  ],
  "openQuestions": [
    {
      "issue": "The current base renders the later eight-terminal A interface from JPH09211645A; the original five-terminal Minolta interface in JPH01302238A is not split into a separate variant.",
      "affectedFields": [
        "mvp.profileModel",
        "contactCount",
        "contacts"
      ],
      "candidateValues": [
        5,
        8
      ],
      "resolution": "Add generation profiles if the application needs original 5-pin Minolta AF bodies/lenses separately."
    },
    {
      "issue": "Fine production clock positions, AF screw-drive coupler position, mount-ring diameter, and screw pattern remain photo-scaled; the bayonet count, electrical contact count/functions, lock/index presence, clockwise engagement, and aperture-control member are patent-backed.",
      "affectedFields": [
        "cameraSideFeatures",
        "lensSideFeatures",
        "contacts",
        "mechanicalCouplings",
        "screwsGasketsBaffles"
      ],
      "candidateValues": [
        "official Minolta/Sony mount drawing",
        "measured production sample"
      ],
      "resolution": "Upgrade remaining photo-scaled values to measured/official when an authoritative production drawing is available."
    }
  ],
  "schemaVersion": "1.3",
  "coordinateConvention": {
    "units": "mm_deg",
    "zDatum": "flange_datum",
    "zPositive": "toward_lens",
    "angleZero": "top_dead_center_camera_front_view",
    "anglePositive": "clockwise_camera_front_view",
    "storedAngleFrame": "camera_front_view",
    "lensRearMirror": "theta_out = (360 - theta) mod 360",
    "angleSpanRule": "clockwise_sweep_start_to_end_mod_360"
  },
  "render": {
    "unitMapping": "1_user_unit_per_mm",
    "marginFraction": 0.1,
    "numericPrecision": 3,
    "views": {
      "cameraSideFront": {
        "viewBox": "-35 -37 70 72"
      },
      "lensSideRear": {
        "viewBox": "-35 -35 70 70"
      },
      "axialSection": {
        "viewBox": "-51 -39 59 74"
      }
    }
  },
  "metadata": {
    "title": "Sony A mount",
    "desc": "Sony A mount — flange focal distance 44.5 mm, nominal throat 49.7 mm.",
    "view": "unknown",
    "profileId": "sony-a/base"
  }
}
```

#### Sources

| Ref | Type | Citation | Archive | Captured | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sa-1` | secondary | "Minolta A-mount system," Wikipedia. Accessed 2026-06-04. | [snapshot](http://web.archive.org/web/20221225232256/https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minolta_A-mount_system) | 2022-12-25 | medium |
| `sa-p1` | patent | US4659203A, "Bayonet mount arrangement." Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/US4659203A/en) | 2026-06-06 | high |
| `sa-p2` | patent | JPH01302238A, "Interchangeable lens exchanging electrical signal with camera main body." Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/JPH01302238A/en) | 2026-06-06 | high |
| `sa-p3` | patent | JPH09211645A, "Bayonet mount device of lens interchangeable camera." Accessed 2026-06-06. | [snapshot](https://patents.google.com/patent/JPH09211645A/en) | 2026-06-06 | high |

#### Open questions

- The current base renders the later eight-terminal A interface from JPH09211645A; the original five-terminal Minolta interface in JPH01302238A is not split into a separate variant. _(Add generation profiles if the application needs original 5-pin Minolta AF bodies/lenses separately.)_
- Fine production clock positions, AF screw-drive coupler position, mount-ring diameter, and screw pattern remain photo-scaled; the bayonet count, electrical contact count/functions, lock/index presence, clockwise engagement, and aperture-control member are patent-backed. _(Upgrade remaining photo-scaled values to measured/official when an authoritative production drawing is available.)_

### `sony-fe` — Sony E

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Mount ID | `sony-fe` |
| Display label | Sony E |
| Project note | Sony E mount; the sony-fe id covers both full-frame FE and APS-C E lenses. |
| Research status | `researched` |
| MVP status | `mvp_complete` |
| Mount mechanism | `bayonet` (lock `sprung_detent`) |
| Base profile | `sony-fe/base` |
| Selected MVP profile | `sony-fe/base` |
| Variant strategy | `base_only` |

#### Base and variant profile matrix

| Profile ID | Type | Applies to | Adds | Removes | Changes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sony-fe/base` | base | all Sony E / FE lenses and bodies (APS-C and full-frame), 2010-present | bayonet lugs; lock pin/notch; mounting index; ten-contact electrical block along the bottom | all mechanical couplings (fully electronic) | — |

#### Dimensional summary

| Field | Value | Unit |
|---|---|---|
| Flange focal distance | 18 (`official`) | mm |
| Nominal throat diameter | 46.1 (`secondary`) | mm |
| Effective clear aperture | 47 (`patent`) | mm |
| Camera mount outer diameter | 54 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Lens mount outer diameter | 52 (`photo_scaled`) | mm |
| Contact count | 10 (`patent`) | count |
| Lock rotation | unknown (`unknown`) | deg |
| Lock direction | unknown (`unknown`) | — |

#### Open questions

- US9392150B2 backs the E/FE body latch/contact-holder/full-frame geometry, but it does not document the production lock pin/notch, mounting-index clock, outer diameters, screw pattern, or exact production lug/contact clocking. _(Upgrade remaining photo-scaled/unknown fields when an authoritative production drawing or measured sample is available.)_

- Contarex bayonet and coupling geometry are photo-scaled. _(Upgrade with Zeiss service drawings or measured examples.)_

## Appendix — JSON Schema reference

The machine block of each mount validates against `src/mounts/lens-mount.schema.json` (schema version 1.3). In this project the contract is enforced through TypeScript (`src/types/mount.ts` + `satisfies MountSpecInput` + `validateMountSpec.ts`); the JSON block is emitted from the typed object, so it conforms by construction.
